name: [BUG-XS-002] TC-XS-002 — Dashboard 401 leaks whether token was present (token-presence enumeration)
description:
**[BUG-XS-002]**

The dashboard API returns different 401 error messages depending on whether an Authorization header was provided, allowing an attacker to enumerate token validity:

- No Authorization header → `"Missing Authorization token"`
- Invalid/expired token   → `"Invalid or expired token"`

**Expected:** A single generic message for all 401 cases, e.g. `"Authentication required"`.

**Impact:** An attacker learns whether their forged token was accepted at the auth layer or simply absent, helping them refine token-guessing attacks. It also confirms that the service separately handles "no token" and "bad token" — useful for bypassing auth probes.
descriptionHtml:
[BUG-XS-002]

The dashboard API returns different 401 error messages depending on whether an Authorization header was provided, allowing an attacker to enumerate token validity:

  • No Authorization header → "Missing Authorization token"
  • Invalid/expired token → "Invalid or expired token"

Expected: A single generic message for all 401 cases, e.g. "Authentication required".

Impact: An attacker learns whether their forged token was accepted at the auth layer or simply absent, helping them refine token-guessing attacks. It also confirms that the service separately handles "no token" and "bad token" — useful for bypassing auth probes.
statusMessage:
Error: [BUG-XS-002] 401 message must be identical whether token is absent or invalid

expect(received).toBe(expected) // Object.is equality

Expected: "Invalid or expired token"
Received: "Missing Authorization token"
